mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust labor market, which supports aggregate demand and corporate earnings, leading to positive equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest PAYEMS value of 133293.0 indicates continued job growth.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in PAYEMS has a rolling 36-month rank correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns of less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate exceeds a neutral threshold, restrictive monetary policy increases the discount rate for future earnings and tightens financial conditions, negatively impacting equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest FEDFUNDS value of 2.79 is above the specified trigger level.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is above 2.5% over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) and decelerating real retail sales (RSAFS) signals a stagflationary environment where consumer purchasing power erodes, leading to lower corporate revenues and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 6-month change in CPIAUCSL > 2% AND 6-month change in RSAFS < 3%
failure_condition: The specified two-factor signal does not produce a statistically significant negative mean forward return (t-test p > 0.05) over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, an increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) from a low base is a leading indicator of an impending recession, causing investors to de-risk portfolios and leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month moving average crosses above its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.2% is relatively low historically but off its cycle lows.
failure_condition: Triggered signals are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Positive growth in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strength in the manufacturing and production sectors of the economy, which translates directly to higher corporate earnings and positive investor sentiment.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest INDPRO value of 118.527 reflects a high level of industrial activity.
failure_condition: The year-over-year percentage change in INDPRO fails to show a positive and significant correlation with 20-day forward returns over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals a contraction in a key cyclical sector of the economy, which has negative wealth effects and is a leading indicator of broader economic slowdowns, thus predicting negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest HOUST value of 1837.0 is historically high, making it vulnerable to a sharp reversal.
failure_condition: Periods following a trigger event do not show a statistically significant lower mean 60-day forward return compared to non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) rises faster than the rate of inflation (CPIAUCSL), increasing real interest rates tighten financial conditions and reduce the attractiveness of equities relative to bonds, causing negative returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in FEDFUNDS > 3-month change in CPIAUCSL
failure_condition: The information coefficient of the real rate change signal remains below 0.03 on a 36-month rolling basis.

mechanism_narrative: A higher-than-expected quarterly Real GDP (GDPC1) print boosts near-term investor sentiment and expectations for corporate earnings, leading to positive short-term equity market performance.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest GDPC1 value of 11078.2 represents a high level of economic output.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return in the week following a GDP release is not statistically different from the average 5-day return in other weeks.
